description: MEAN-REVERTING regime: apply_regime_exit_adjustment raises the confirmation ticks above the base EXIT_CONFIRM_TICKS, expressing RESTRAINT (harder to confirm = fewer exits on noise, consistent with Kaminski-Lo 2014 mean-reverting -> stops are neutral-to-harmful).

derivation.spec_basis: PHASE3_DECISIONS.md §Regime->response mapping: MEAN-REVERTING -> RESTRAINT (hold through noise, exits LESS). 00-ADAPTIVE-RECOMMENDATION.md §1A: 'the measured intraday regime is mean-reverting, where Kaminski-Lo says stops are neutral-to-harmful. An honest adaptive layer will most often counsel restraint (stand down / widen), not more.' Kaminski-Lo (2014): returns REVERSE in mean-reverting regimes, so a triggered stop is more likely a noise tick than a genuine trend break. More confirmation ticks filter out reversions before exit fires.
derivation.direction: adjusted_ticks > base_ticks (strictly more for mean-reverting = restraint)
derivation.tolerance_note: Expected value is an integer. The test asserts DIRECTION (greater than base) and upper-bound safety. The exact value is determined by MEAN_REVERTING_EXIT_TICKS named constant in math_engine.py.

expected.comment: adjusted_ticks must be > base_ticks=3. The exact value is determined by the named constant MEAN_REVERTING_EXIT_TICKS in math_engine.py. There is a hard upper bound test elsewhere.
